name: @abtree/srp-refactor

steps.instruct: Score the codebase for Single Responsibility Principle violations. For every non-trivial module/file you assess, capture: - path - responsibilities mixed (list of distinct concerns the file owns) - severity (low | medium | high | critical) - one-line rationale (why those concerns shouldn't co-exist here) Save the list ranked most-critical-first to $LOCAL.violations.
From $LOCAL.violations, write the top-ranked entry to $LOCAL.top_violation. Set $LOCAL.has_critical_violations to true if any entry has severity "high" or "critical", else false.

Compose a markdown SRP report with these sections: # SRP report — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> ## Summary (one paragraph: count of critical/high, the top concern, and whether the previously chosen violation — if $LOCAL.chosen_violation is set — is now resolved) ## Ranked violations (table: Path | Mixed responsibilities | Severity | Rationale — rendered from $LOCAL.violations) ## Top violation (rendered from $LOCAL.top_violation, with quoted code snippets where it clarifies the mix of concerns) Write the markdown to ./SRP_REPORT.md (overwriting any prior copy). Save the path to $LOCAL.srp_report.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.srp_report is set and $LOCAL.violations is set and $LOCAL.has_critical_violations is set
$LOCAL.violations is set and $LOCAL.has_critical_violations is set and $LOCAL.srp_report is set

steps.instruct: Capture the initial scoring state so the final Change_Report can show before-vs-after. Each loop iteration of Score_SRP overwrites $LOCAL.violations / $LOCAL.top_violation / $LOCAL.has_critical_violations and the ./SRP_REPORT.md file, so this snapshot is the only record of where the codebase started. Do all of the following: - copy $LOCAL.violations → $LOCAL.initial_violations - copy $LOCAL.top_violation → $LOCAL.initial_top_violation - copy $LOCAL.has_critical_violations → $LOCAL.initial_has_critical_violations - copy the file at $LOCAL.srp_report to ./SRP_REPORT_INITIAL.md (overwriting any prior copy) and save the new path to $LOCAL.initial_srp_report.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.initial_violations is set and $LOCAL.initial_has_critical_violations is set and $LOCAL.initial_srp_report is set

steps.instruct: Show the human the SRP report at $LOCAL.srp_report and ask which violation they want to tackle. The human picks by writing their answer to $LOCAL.chosen_violation via: abtree local write <execution-id> chosen_violation "<file path or label>" While waiting, submit `running` to ack-and-pause without advancing the cursor. Do NOT submit success until $LOCAL.chosen_violation is a non-empty string.

Refactor the codebase to resolve $LOCAL.chosen_violation. Split the mixed responsibilities into focused units, update callers, and preserve observable behaviour. If $LOCAL.refactor_summary is already set, this is a re-attempt — read the previous summary, identify what didn't fully land, and improve on it rather than starting over. When done, overwrite $LOCAL.refactor_summary with a short markdown summary of what moved where (bullet list of files/symbols changed, one line each). Set $LOCAL.refactor_complete to true.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.refactor_complete is true and $LOCAL.refactor_summary is a non-empty string

steps.instruct: Score the codebase for Single Responsibility Principle violations. For every non-trivial module/file you assess, capture: - path - responsibilities mixed (list of distinct concerns the file owns) - severity (low | medium | high | critical) - one-line rationale (why those concerns shouldn't co-exist here) Save the list ranked most-critical-first to $LOCAL.violations.
From $LOCAL.violations, write the top-ranked entry to $LOCAL.top_violation. Set $LOCAL.has_critical_violations to true if any entry has severity "high" or "critical", else false.

Compose a markdown SRP report with these sections: # SRP report — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> ## Summary (one paragraph: count of critical/high, the top concern, and whether the previously chosen violation — if $LOCAL.chosen_violation is set — is now resolved) ## Ranked violations (table: Path | Mixed responsibilities | Severity | Rationale — rendered from $LOCAL.violations) ## Top violation (rendered from $LOCAL.top_violation, with quoted code snippets where it clarifies the mix of concerns) Write the markdown to ./SRP_REPORT.md (overwriting any prior copy). Save the path to $LOCAL.srp_report.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.srp_report is set and $LOCAL.violations is set and $LOCAL.has_critical_violations is set

steps.instruct: No critical SRP violations remain. Note this in the trace and proceed to final review.

Run the code-review procedure documented at `fragments/code-review.md`. Read that file and follow its numbered steps verbatim, scoping the review to the changes captured in $LOCAL.refactor_summary (the diff produced during this session). The procedure orchestrates haiku/sonnet/opus subagents in parallel and ends with a filtered, high-signal issue list. Capture that final list (issue description + reason) as $LOCAL.review_report. If the list is empty, write `[]` — an empty list is a valid result.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.initial_violations is set and $LOCAL.violations is set and $LOCAL.refactor_summary is a non-empty string

steps.instruct: Compose a markdown change report comparing the initial SRP score to the post-refactor score. Required sections: # SRP change report — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> ## Headline - Critical violations: <count in initial_violations> → <count in violations> - High violations: <count in initial_violations> → <count in violations> - Total violations: <count in initial_violations> → <count in violations> - Chosen target: $LOCAL.chosen_violation - Resolved? yes if $LOCAL.initial_top_violation no longer appears in $LOCAL.violations (compare by path), else no. ## Refactor summary $LOCAL.refactor_summary (verbatim, fenced as a code block) ## Score before Table: Path | Severity | Mixed responsibilities — rendered from $LOCAL.initial_violations. ## Score after Table: Path | Severity | Mixed responsibilities — rendered from $LOCAL.violations. If the list is empty, write "No remaining violations." ## Reports on disk - Initial: $LOCAL.initial_srp_report - Final: $LOCAL.srp_report - Review: inline below ## Final code review $LOCAL.review_report (verbatim) Write the markdown to ./SRP_CHANGE_REPORT.md (overwriting any prior copy). Save the path to $LOCAL.change_report.

description: Score the codebase for Single Responsibility violations, snapshot the initial scoring, pause for the human to pick one to tackle, refactor it in a bounded loop (re-scoring after every pass), do a multi-agent code review, and finish with a before-vs-after change report.
